channel.title: Women Talk Sci Fi
channel.description: The girls of SciFi, Gerri and Eugenia, from GE News Ezine now bring you Women Talk Sci Fi. A Podcast by women about their favourite genre, Sci-Fi. You've read what we have to say now you can listen to us too!
channel.itunes:author: Gerri & Eugenia
channel.itunes:keywords: women, talk, sci-fi, scifi, sci, fi, podcast
channel.itunes:subtitle: Because women like to talk
channel.itunes:summary: The girls of SciFi, Gerri and Eugenia, from GE News Ezine now bring you Women Talk Sci Fi. A Podcast by women about their favourite genre, Sci-Fi. You've read what we have to say now you can listen to us too!

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 141 - Pokemon Is Go! Veronica Taylor and Rena Taylor
item.description: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri from the world of Pokemon interview Veronica Taylor who was the voice of Ash Ketchum for the first 8 seasons. Along with her daughter Rena they do a podcast called The Trainer's Guide. [more]
item.author: contact@womentalkscifi.com (Gerri & Eugenia)
item.itunes:author: Gerri & Eugenia
item.itunes:summary: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri from the world of Pokemon interview Veronica Taylor who was the voice of Ash Ketchum for the first 8 seasons. Along with her daughter Rena they do a podcast called The Trainer's Guide.

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 140 - Star Trek Just Never Goes Away
item.description: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri have four interviews with actors from the Star Trek franchise. Tony Todd, Patricia Tallman, Daniel Davis and Nicole de Boer. This is an extra special episode for their tenth anniversary. [more]
item.author: contact@womentalkscifi.com (Gerri & Eugenia)
item.itunes:author: Gerri & Eugenia
item.itunes:summary: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri have four interviews with actors from the Star Trek franchise. Tony Todd, Patricia Tallman, Daniel Davis and Nicole de Boer. This is an extra special episode for their tenth anniversary.

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 139 - Any Who Will Do - Pearl Mackie
item.description: For their Tenth Anniversary year in this podcast Gerri and Eugenia have four interviews from Doctor Who actors that span almost 50 years of the show. They are John Levene Sgt Benson, Geoffrey Beevers The Master, Catrin Stewart Jenny Flint and the wonderful Pearl Mackie Bill Potts. [more]
item.author: contact@womentalkscifi.com (Gerri & Eugenia)
item.itunes:author: Gerri & Eugenia
item.itunes:summary: For their Tenth Anniversary year in this podcast Gerri and Eugenia have four interviews from Doctor Who actors that span almost 50 years of the show. They are John Levene Sgt Benson, Geoffrey Beevers The Master, Catrin Stewart Jenny Flint and the wonderful Pearl Mackie Bill Potts.

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 138 - What a Whovian Can Do - Gary Russell
item.description: Eugenia and Gerri interview the ultimate Doctor Who fan Gary Russell. Gary took is obsession with Doctor Who from dreaming to working in many field of the Doctor Who franchise. Editor of the Doctor Who Magazine, head of Big Finish, author, script editor on Doctor Who, as well as working on the Sarah Jane Adventures, Gary has done it all. [more]
item.author: contact@womentalkscifi.com (Gerri & Eugenia)

item.itunes:summary: Eugenia and Gerri interview the ultimate Doctor Who fan Gary Russell. Gary took is obsession with Doctor Who from dreaming to working in many field of the Doctor Who franchise. Editor of the Doctor Who Magazine, head of Big Finish, author, script editor on Doctor Who, as well as working on the Sarah Jane Adventures, Gary has done it all.

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 137 - From Old Wolf to Teen Wolf
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia have not one, not two but 4 interviews in this podcast as they talk about werewolves. They interview David Naughton from An American Werewolf in London and cast members from Teen Wolf. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 136 - It's Still Cold Outside - Norman Lovett - Craig Charles
item.description: Its still cold outside no kind of atmosphere. Eugenia and Gerri interview another cast member from Red Dwarf, Norman Lovett who played Holly the Computer. But wait theres more; during the interview Craig Charles (Lister) audio bombed the discussion and a laugh fest begins. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 124 - The People Who Make the Magic
item.description: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri talk about the people who make the magic happen on the screen, the make-up artists, prop makers and stunt co-ordinators. They interview from WETA make-up artist extraordinaire Warren Dion Smith and prop painter Mark Dewes. Eugenia and Gerri also interview stunt co-ordinator Richard Norton. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 122 - We Talk Live from AMC Expo
item.description: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri record live from AMC Expo Melbourne. Four surprise guests arrive to talk to them. Dorian Gregory from Charmed, Hunter Page-Lochard from Australias Sci Fi show Cleverman, a real spaceman, Space Shuttle astronaut Rick Searfoss and Super Villian Dr Light, Malese Jow from The Flash, and that is not all. Listen for more interviews and a song by Geeky singer Meri Amber. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 100 - Star Trek DS9 Brooks Visitor Eisenberg and Grodenchik interviews for you

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 74 - Minister of Chance - McCoy McGann Brophy
item.description: Eugenia and Gerri talk about The Minister of Chance a FREE audio drama written and directed by Dan Freeman. They interview not one, not two but three of the cast, Sylvester McCoy (the Seventh Doctor) who plays the Witch Prime, Paul McGann (the Eighth Doctor) who plays Durian and Jed Brophy (Noki, Dwarf from The Hobbit) plays Pilot. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 73 - The Geek From Warehouse 13 - Eddie McClintock
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia interview in this podcast the funny and whacky Eddie McClintock who plays the loveable Pete Lattimer in Warehouse 13. The writers of Warehouse 13 have come up with a winning formula allowing them to take an object from any time, any place and any person in history and do a story about them. It is a cross between Indiana Jones, cop shows, and a "who-dun it", with the odd supernatural mix. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 72 - Star Trek Into Darkness - First Look
item.description: Eugenia and Gerri are on the red carpet for the Australian Premiere and first showing in the world of the new Star Trek movie Into Darkness. They interview director JJ Abrams, Karl Urban and Chris Pine then discuss their impressions of the film. No spoilers. So listen and then go out and buy a ticket to watch the movie. You will not regret it. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 71 - Red Dwarf - It's Cold Outside - Craig Charles
item.description: The British are very good at keeping a television show alive for a long time look at Doctor Who and many other soaps and programs that stay around for many years. But in the world of British geekdom there is another iconic program and that is Red Dwarf. Gerri and Eugenia talk about Red Dwarf and the heart and soul of the show Dave Lister, played by none other than Craig Charles who they interview this episode. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 69 - Every Ben 10 needs a Grandpa Max - Paul Eiding
item.description: In this episode Gerri and Eugenia get into cartoons. A genre we all love no matter what age we are. Ben 10 has graced the TV of many of us either as a watcher or by osmosis when the kids have it on the TV and it has been around for at least 7 years. They interview Paul Eiding the voice of Grandpa Max. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 68 - The Boldest of Companions - Louise Jameson
item.description: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri along with guest presenter Jo Shrapnel discuss what we all want to be and that is a Doctor Who companion. Not only do they talk about those that have gone before them, they try to define what constitutes being a companion. The interview in this podcast is Louise Jameson, whose character Leela a Sevateem savage travelled with the 4th Doctor. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 67 - Superman versus Zod - Callum Blue
item.description: Every woman wants him and Gerri and Eugenia are no different. They are talking about ...is it a bird, is it a plane no its Superman yes, the man of steel himself. They discuss the many incarnations of the man of steel from the comic books to the movies to the TV shows.They also get to talk with very funny Callum Blue who played Major Zod in Smallville. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 56 - Kavan Smith and his Eureka Moments
item.description: Eureka, Eugenia and Gerri found it! Well the topic for this podcast that is! Eugenia and Gerri had the pleasure of catching up with the robotic Deputy Andy himself, Kavan Smith.They talk about the aspects they like about the show and what hopes they have for its final episodes. Kavan gives insight into the world of Eureka and how he developed his robot character. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 55 - A Little Bit of Magic - Barbara Eden
item.description: In this podcast Gerri and Eugenia cast a little bit of magic. They discuss the use of magic in TV shows. They get to interview none other than Jeannie herself, Barbara Eden and if that is not magic all on its own they also talk with Larry Hagman.[more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 54 - The Best of the Baddies - Cliff Simon
item.description: Eugenia and Gerri go back to one of their favourites, Stargate. They talk about all the baddies that battled with Stargate Command over the years of the shows. They discuss the ones that they liked even though they shouldn't. They talk about one particular baddie that they both like very, Ba'al.[more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 53 - Bill Farmer - A Little Bit Goofy
item.description: In this podcast Gerri and Eugenia get to interview one of the worlds best-known voices! It is Bill Farmer the legendary voice of Goofy and Pluto some of Disneys most enduring characters. Recently inducted into the Disney hall of fame for his 25 years of voicing his characters he is one of Disneys Legends. [more]

item.itunes:summary: In this podcast Gerri and Eugenia get to interview one of the worlds best-known voices! It is Bill Farmer the legendary voice of Goofy and Pluto some of Disneys most enduring characters.

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 52 - B Grades Can Be Brilliant - Corin Nemec
item.description: The world of B grade Sci Fi movies is the topic of discussion in this podcast. Eugenia and Gerri talk about memorable movies that dont quite make the A list of movies or even the C and D lists! In a very chatty interview with Corin, Eugenia and Gerri find out a lot about his many interests and experiences being part of a growing television industry. [more]
item.author: contact@womentalkscifi.com (Gerri & Eugenia)
item.itunes:author: Gerri & Eugenia
item.itunes:summary: The world of B grade Sci Fi movies is the topic of discussion in this podcast. Eugenia and Gerri talk about memorable movies that dont quite make the A list of movies or even the C and D lists! However there is one movie that they both rave about and that is Sand Sharks produced by and starring Corin Nemec In a very chatty interview with Corin, Eugenia and Gerri find out a lot about his many interests and experiences being part of a growing television industry.

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 51 - The Two Faces of Chuck Campbell in Sanctuary
item.description: In this podcast Gerri and Eugenia catch up with Chuck Campbell. Chuck has starred in many roles within the Sci Fi genre. He starred as Chuck the Gate Guy on Stargate Atlantis and Two Faced in Sanctuary, but, what you may not realise is that behind the scenes of both shows he plays a much bigger role! Gerri and Eugenia along with guest presenter Kess, talk about their thoughts of why Sanctuary is a TV show to watch![more]

Gerri and Eugenia along with guest presenter Kess, talk about their thoughts of why Sanctuary is a TV show to watch!

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 50 - Amanda Tapping - A Woman Talking Sci Fi
item.description: Its Eugenia and Gerris 50th podcast!! Their special guest interview for this podcast is none other than iconic Sci Fi actor Amanda Tapping. Amanda talks about her commitment to Sanctuary from the webisodes to TV show. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 49 - From Soldier to Gourmet - Colin Cunningham
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia interview Colin Cunningham at Adelaide Armageddon just before the television series Falling Skies aired. Most of us know Colin as Major Davis from Stargate SG-1, but he now stars as John Pope in Falling Skies from Steven Spielberg. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 48 - Behind the Scenes - Judy Ducker
item.description: Eugenia and Gerri find out what GI Joe, The Golden Compass, Snow White and the Huntsman and Doctor Who, movies and shows have in common. So will you when you listen to an entertaining and interesting interview with one of the industries top Production Buyers, Judy Ducker. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 47 - We Meet the King Of Vampires - Denis O'Hare
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia are joined by guest presenter Kess to discuss the television series True Blood. This lively conversation has the trio divided as to whether Eric is a cut above the other TV/movie vampires. Gerri and Eugenia had the pleasure of interviewing Denis OHare who plays Russell Edgington, vampire King of Mississippi. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 46 - Space Can Be A Funny Place - Michael Winslow
item.description: Eugenia and Gerri talk about their favourite Sci Fi parodies such as Galaxy Quest and Spaceballs and the humour of movies and TV shows that have parodied Sci Fi and Fantasy and those that give homage to Sci Fi in a fun way. The guest interviewed by Eugenia and Gerri is this episode is the talented Michael Winslow who is probably more recognised as the Human Beat Box Larvelle Jones from the Police Academy movies but has also appeared in Spaceballs and Gremlins [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 45 - SG1 Baddie Apophis Talks
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia talk about Sci Fi villains due to interviewing Peter Williams who starred as Apophis in Stargate. You hear Peter talk about his time on SG-1 and how he set the bar for the other System Lords. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 44 - Sci Fi or Not?
item.description: Eugenia and Gerri talk about a slightly different show than they normally do The Goodies, a 1970-80s comedy show starring one of the UKs most famous comedy teams. Why are Women Talk Sci Fi talking about it? They think it is a Sci Fi show and in this episode They convince Jeff from The Goodies Podcast that it is! [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 43 - Robots and Other Things - Brent Spiner
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia talk about robots, androids and the odd dalek or two. They look at the history of the machine man/woman from the first in Sci Fi films the newly released and remastered, Metropolis from madman.com.au to today. They have an interview with one of the genres most loved character actors, the engaging and fascinating Brent Spiner who played the android Data in Star Trek The Next Generation! Thanks go to Scott and Diana Liston from First Contact Conventions [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 42 - Fan Sites to Fan Fiction
item.description: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri interview Herb Jefferson Jr., Boomer from the original Battlestar Galactica TV series but, that is not what this podcast is about. It is about Star Trek fan fiction films. What is the link between Herb and Star Trek fan fiction? You will have to listen and the connection will become clear. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 41 - Tailwags from John Leeson
item.description: In this podcast Gerri and Eugenia have a conversation with someone special to them! John Leeson the voice of K9 and the Doctors longest running companion. K9 has worked with more incarnations of the Doctor than any other companion has! John Leeson played the voice of K9 for most of the last 25 years and in this podcast we get to listen to some of his most memorable moments from the set of the show, his career in the arts and his passion for food and wine. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 40 - All the Goodies of Gallifrey One
item.description: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri are joined by Jeff one of the presenters from the Goodies Podcast and his good friend Michael. They attended the Gallifrey Ones Catch 22 Inlands of Mystery Doctor Who Convention in Los Angeles. The conventions have been running for 20 years and always boast a large line up of Doctor Who guests. Now is your chance to hear firsthand what it is like to attend such a big event and even hear from some of the guests they met during the Con. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 39 - Lt Reed From Armoury Man to Funny Man
item.description: At a First Contact Convention in Melbourne last year Gerri and Eugenia had the pleasure of interviewing Dominic Keating who played Lt Malcolm Reed in Star Trek Enterprise. The two of them explore the highs and lows of this Trek incarnation discussing its beginning and end. For an added treat you get to hear Dominics alternative version of the Enterprise theme. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 38 - Lord of the Rings - Gamling Talks
item.description: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri go a bit Epic! Lord of the Rings Epic that is! They discuss the J R Tolkiens epic series and Peter Jacksons movies. What do they really think? Are they true ring fans? What they are is very lucky in this podcast they get to interview one of New Zealands LOTR actors none other Bruce Hopkins who played Gamling, lieutenant to King Théoden. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 37 - The Incredible Hulk-Lou Ferrigno
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia go a bit retro in this podcast. They look at the different incarnations of the green-bodied Incredible Hulk and yes the interview in this podcast is with none other than, The Incredible Hulk Lou Ferrigno from the TV series of the same name. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 36 - Anime Not Just for the Young
item.description: In this podcast the world of Anime is explored with Eugenia and Gerris guest presenters Robyn and Deanna. As Eugenia and Gerri are new to the world of Anime, Robyn and Deanna talk about how they became involved in Anime and what they like about the genre. At Armageddon Melbourne last year Eugenia and Gerri had the pleasure of interviewing Mela Lee who is the voice, Yuki Cross from Vampire Knight. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 35 - A Little Byte of Angel - Mark Lutz Interview
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia are joined by guest geeks Robyn and Deanna to discuss the TV series Angel (and some of the boys in it). The topics vary from plot line to the impact of the characters on the show. They also have an interview with Mark Lutz who played The Groosalugg on Angel. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 34 - The Place is Babylon 5 - Claudia Christian
item.description: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri discuss the television series Babylon 5 and talk about the differences between Babylon 5 and other shows. Yes, there is an interview and due to popular demand it is Claudia Christian, who played Susan Ivanova in the series talks about her time on the show and her current projects. [more]

item.description: You have listened to the many interviews that Gerri and Eugenia have done over the last two years of the podcast from stars of their favourite movies and TV shows to authors and moviemakers. In this podcast they do it a little differently, they get interviewed. However they are not just interviewed by anyone, but by Bruce Hopkins, Gamling, from The Lord of the Rings movies and New Zealand radio personality [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 32 - Harry Potter the Magic Continues
item.description: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri delve into the world of Wizards and Magic as they discuss the phenomenon of Harry Potter. They talk of their favourite characters, the impact the story of Harry Potter has had on the world and how creative is the mind of J.K. Rowling. They also bring out of the vault an interview with Chris Rankin who plays Percy Weasley.[more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 31 - Buffy the Vampire Slayer and Larry
item.description: In this podcast Gerri and Eugenia talk about Buffy the Vampire Slayer, one of the most innovative shows in the Sci Fi/Fantasy genre!You also get to a listen to an interview they did at a Supanova Convention with Larry Bagby, who of course played Larry the Jock in Buffy. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 30 - The Counsellor Is In - Marina Sirtis
item.description: This podcast by Eugenia and Gerri is the last in their trilogy of Star Trek: The Next Generation icons (for now anyway). They had the pleasure of talking to Marina Sirtis who played Counsellor Deanna Troi at First Contact Conventions Oz Trek 3.[more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 29 - Michael Dorn - A Klingon Encounter
item.description: Eugenia and Gerri in this podcast get to interview the man who has appeared in more Star Trek episodes and movies than any other actor. It is none other than Michael Dorn who played the Klingon warrior Worf from Star Trek: The Next Generation and Deep Space Nine. This is part 2 of their TNG interview specials and they were very excited to get the chance to speak to one of the most recognised characters from all the Star Trek Series. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 28 - LeVar Burton-A Man Who Kept the USS Enterprise Flying
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia got to interview not only one of Star Trek: The Next Generation icons, but three of them. In this first interview of a three part The Next Generation series they interview LeVar Burton otherwise known as Geordi LaForge, the Chief Engineer of the USS Enterprise. They also discuss the Chief Engineers of all the Star Trek incarnations. [more]

In this first interview of a three part The Next Generation series they interview LeVar Burton otherwise known as Geordi LaForge, the Chief Engineer of the USS Enterprise.

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 27 - David Hewlett Down Under
item.description: This podcast is a little different. Gerri and Eugenia went to a convention at which the inimitable Stargate Atlantis actor David Hewlett who played Dr Meredith Rodney McKay was one of the guests. David is a great showman and kept the audience well entertained, so we thought that you might like to hear him too. He talks about his role with the show, his love of the UK TV show Doctor Who and the movie that he wrote, directed, and starred in A Dogs Breakfast. What are you waiting for start downloading it!! [more]
item.author: contact@womentalkscifi.com (Gerri & Eugenia)
item.itunes:author: Gerri & Eugenia
item.itunes:summary: Gerri and Eugenia attended a convention with the inimitable Stargate Atlantis actor David Hewlett, Dr Meredith Rodney McKay. David is a great showman, he kept the audience entertained, so we thought that you might like to hear him too. So get listening!

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 26 - Star Wars - Darth Vader Grows Up
item.description: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri get to talk to an incarnation of one of the worlds best-known villains! They get to interview Darth Vader, well the young Anakin Skywalker played by Jake Lloyd. So have a listen sit back and find out how he got the part and what he is doing now. They also interview Sean Williams, New York Times best selling author of Star Wars: The Force Unleashed and his latest Star Wars: The Old Alliance: Fatal Republic who also happens to be from their hometown of Adelaide, South Australia. Eugenia and Gerri also discuss what impact George Lucass Star Wars epic series has had on Sci Fi. As you have heard in previous podcasts Gerri and Eugenia are from Adelaide. [more]

Eugenia and Gerri also discuss what impact George Lucass Star Wars epic series has had on Sci Fi.

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 25 - Dichen The Doll Talks Dollhouse
item.description: As you have heard in previous podcasts Gerri and Eugenia are from Adelaide, Australia and they were lucky enough recently to interview another Adelaide girl who has made it big in the US. The show they discuss is Dollhouse and the Adelaide girl is self confessed Geek Girl, Dichen Lachman who plays Sierra, one of the Actives or Dolls who have their mind wiped taking on the personality of another to complete missions. [more]

They discuss whether this show a new concept with its premise or has it been done before?

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 24 - Star Trek Aliens - An Interview with Rene and Ethan
item.description: This has to be the funniest podcast Eugenia and Gerri have recorded to date. They caught up with Rene Auberjonois and Ethan Phillips at The Hub Productions Convention in Adelaide, Australia. In this interview they had trouble keeping their giggles low and almost forgot to ask questions. Listen and find out why. you will enjoy it! [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 23 - Ianto Jones - From Coffee Maker to Alien Hunter
item.description: Ianto Jones, the man in the suit from Torchwood is the topic of this podcast. Gerri and Eugenia had the privilege of interviewing Gareth David-Lloyd at Melbourne Supanova this year. In this podcast Gerri and Eugenia talk about the development of Iantos character from coffee maker to action hero during the three seasons of Torchwood. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 22 - Full Moon or Twilight Brings Out the Werewolf
item.description: In this podcast Eugenia and Gerri discuss the evolution of the werewolf in movies and TV from 1935 to present day but they also get to talk to one. At Supanova www.supanova.com.au Eugenia and Gerri chat with Alex Meraz, Paul one of the wolf pack from Twilight: New Moon and Eclipse. They also speak to Bret Kennedy and Anthony Edwards discussing a new Australian Sci Fi movie called The Dark Lurking www.thedarklurkingmovie.com [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 21 - Women Talk Sci Fi
item.description: In this their 21st podcast, Gerri and Eugenia have an all Women Talk Sci Fi podcast! At The Hub Productions Star Trek Convention in Adelaide www.thehubproductions.com , they got to interview Denise Crosby their first female actor interview. Eugenia and Gerri discuss the impact her character had on the show and how in her portrayal of Tasha Yar Denise made it easy to imagine that women who were capable could be responsible for the security of a great big ship. [more]

item.itunes:summary: In this their 21st podcast, Gerri and Eugenia have an all Women Talk Sci FI podcast! At The Hub Productions Star Trek Convention in Adelaide www.thehubproductions.com , they got to interview Denise Crosby their first female actor interview.

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 20 - And The Doctor Will See You Now
item.description: Eugenia and Gerri are celebrating not only their one-year anniversary in this episode but also hitting the twenties! To celebrate their birthday and the new Season of Doctor Who (a definite favourite of the pair) they talk about the new Doctor Who, Matt Smith and review the new series so far. But they also have a special treat for all Doctor Who fans listening as they got to interview Paul McGann the 8th Doctor recently at First Contact Conventions, Melbourne Triocon. [more]
item.author: contact@womentalkscifi.com (Gerri & Eugenia)
item.itunes:author: Gerri & Eugenia
item.itunes:summary: Eugenia and Gerri are celebrating their one-year anniversary. During this podcast they talk about the new series of Doctor Who and their special interview is Paul McGann the 8th Doctor.

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 19 - Through the Stargate to Atlantis
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia and their two good friends Marlene and Sarah went to an Armageddon Convention in Melbourne. This was Sarahs first time at a convention and she attended because most of the guests were from Stargate Atlantis. So they decided to all get together and talk about the show. [more]

item.itunes:author: Gerri & Eugenia
item.itunes:summary: Gerri and Eugenia are joined by guest presenters Marlene and Sarah as they discuss their views about Stargate Atlantis.

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 18 - The Day We Knew We Were Truly 'Geek'
item.description: Eugenia and Gerri joined by guest presenter Marlene recall their first experiences of going to a Sci Fi convention. You will hear their excitement, their fears and their memories of that special first time when they went from geek to Geek! [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 17 - Avatar - The Shape of Things to Come
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia both went to see Avatar at different session times (the first time) and now they are sitting down to talk about their experience. They discuss the storyline of Avatar, the 3D technology, the visual effects and the impact this movie will have on the movie industry. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 16 - You Know it is True because He is Doctor Who.
item.description: Eugenia and Gerri give you something a little different! Eugenia is an avid yes avid Dr Who fan and well although Gerri loves it too, she was not lucky enough to get to go to the convention that starred the fifth Doctor, even though he is Gerris favourite. What you will hear is Peter at a convention where he is asked the questions that fans want to know! [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 15 - Do Not Feed The Doctor He Regenerates.
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia are avid Doctor Who fans and have been for many a year. Consequently they have witnessed a few of the regenerations of the various Doctors over the years. In this podcast Gerri and Eugenia discuss their reactions about the many faces of the Doctor. [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 14 - Every Girl Loves A Bad Boy
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia just love a bad boy and in this podcast they tell who they think are the bad boys of Sci Fi check out and see if you agree? They have an interview with one of their favourite bad boys Starbuck from the original 1978 Battlestar Galactica series! [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 13 - A Bloodsucking Podcast
item.description: With the Twilight craze that has bitten the world at the moment, Eugenia and Gerri talk about the TV world of the Vampire. From their extensive research (OK lots of TV and movie watching) they know how Vampires should behave, how they should look and what they should be allowed to do. Check them out and see if you agree with them? [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 12 - Torchwood: The End of Days or Not?
item.description: Gerri and Eugenia discuss the three seasons of Torchwood in this episode. By the end of their discussion they begin to wonder why they like this show!! So what are their views about the characters of Jack, Gwen, Owen, Tosh and Ianto? What do they really think about the last season Children of the Earth? Listen with them and recall the many characters that have died, interesting storylines and the situations that the characters find themselves in! [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 11 - Stargate Battlestar Voyager The New Universe
item.description: With both Stargates off the air Gerri and Eugenia were looking forward to Universe. Having seen the promos they wonder if it could be a version of Battle Stargate Universe. They so wished that it could stand alone! What do they think of the new series? Download this episode and see if they nailed it? [more]

item.title: Women Talk Sci Fi - Episode 06 - An Apple a Day Keeps the Doctor Away
item.description: Join Gerri and Eugenia as they reminisce about Doctor Whos fifth reincarnation Peter Davison and his various companions. Somebody has been eating there apples though. We don't have a Doctor to interview but we do have a companion. Listen to the very funny, and very intelligent, Mark Stricksen as he talks about his career. [more]
